title: angelica saxatilis
intro: Angelica saxatilis, also known as American angelica or wild angelica, is a perennial plant native to North America. It is a member of the carrot family and has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to relieve pain, reduce fever, and support the immune system, making it a popular natural remedy for colds, flu, and other infections. Angelica saxatilis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing individuals to harness its medicinal properties and reap its numerous health benefits.
